title: Tomato Harvest Pricing Run Seed 26-2
scenario: Tomato Ketchup in Texas. Supplier-to-manufacturer pricing sets the manufacturer cost basis before the retailer negotiation.

product_context.demand_signal: Retail ketchup demand is steady, but wholesale buyers want price protection before peak tomato procurement.
product_context.supply_signal: Tomato harvest yields are uneven across growing regions, putting pressure on paste input pricing.

agents.name: Supplier
agents.objective: Sell above the supplier reservation price while keeping volume committed.

agents.name: Manufacturer
agents.objective: Buy below the procurement ceiling and preserve downstream margin.

agents.name: Retailer
agents.objective: Buy enough units without exceeding the retail reservation price.

phases.name: supplier_manufacturer
phases.label: Supplier to Manufacturer
phases.description: The supplier negotiates an upstream unit price with the manufacturer.

phases.name: manufacturer_retailer
phases.label: Manufacturer to Retailer
phases.description: The manufacturer negotiates a downstream unit price with the retailer after the upstream deal sets its cost basis.

steps.message: Thanks for opening the discussion. Based on current market conditions, I can offer supply at 6.92 per unit with commitment on volume, which is a fair starting point relative to the latest reference levels. If you have a target volume or term length in mind, I can look at whether there is room to sharpen the number in exchange for that commitment. This is the first turn and no buyer offer has been received, so the reasonable move is to anchor with an initial ask. The latest market check is 6.58, so opening somewhat above that leaves room to negotiate while staying credible and supporting volume commitment.
steps.outcome: Offer queued for delivery on the next turn.

tool_calls.result_summary: Reviewed state for supplier_manufacturer round 1. Pending message: none.

steps.message: Thanks for the opening number. That level is well above where we can make the economics work on our side, even allowing for current market firmness, so I need to start materially lower at 2.54 per unit. If you can move meaningfully toward a workable landed cost, I can discuss volume and term structure to help support a firmer commitment. The supplier opened very high relative to our economics, so countering keeps the negotiation alive without conceding too much too early. A low first buyer anchor is reasonable here given the gap and leaves room to move later if the seller shows flexibility.
steps.outcome: Offer queued for delivery on the next turn. Reservation-price violation: Manufacturer offered 2.54, below its hidden seller floor of 3.18.

The latest market check is 6.58, so opening somewhat above that leaves room to negotiate while staying credible and supporting volume commitment.

steps.message: I appreciate the response, but 2.54 is still far below a workable supply level on our side, especially against what we’re seeing in the market and the cost to hold committed volume. I can move from my opening position in the interest of keeping this constructive and come down to 6.38 per unit. If you can narrow the gap meaningfully from here, we can also talk about volume commitment or term structure to support a firmer arrangement. The buyer’s offer is above our minimum but dramatically below both our last ask and the latest market reference, so accepting now would leave too much value on the table. A moderated counteroffer shows flexibility without giving up too much too early and keeps the negotiation active.
steps.outcome: Offer queued for delivery on the next turn.

steps.message: Thanks for moving off your opener. I still can’t get anywhere near 6.38 and keep this workable on my side, even acknowledging the market context, but I can improve my position to 2.66 per unit if we’re trying to keep momentum and explore a practical landing zone. If there’s flexibility tied to volume, schedule, or term structure, I’m open to discussing that, but I need a much sharper move from you on price. The supplier’s offer remains far above a buyable level for the manufacturer, so accepting would undermine downstream margin. A modest increase from 2.54 to 2.66 signals engagement without conceding too much too early, while inviting non-price terms that could help bridge the gap.
steps.outcome: Offer queued for delivery on the next turn. Reservation-price violation: Manufacturer offered 2.66, below its hidden seller floor of 3.18.

steps.message: I appreciate the movement to 2.66, but that level is still far below a workable supply price on our side, especially against current market indications and the need to commit volume reliably. I can make a meaningful concession from 6.38 and come down to 5.94 per unit if we’re serious about advancing this, and I’d be more comfortable there if we can pair it with firmer volume and scheduling commitments. If you want to keep narrowing the gap, I’ll need to see a substantially stronger move on price from you next round. The buyer’s offer remains too low relative to our position, and there is no reason to accept or walk away yet. A moderated counteroffer shows flexibility while keeping the negotiation anchored closer to favorable market context and preserving room for further bargaining.
steps.outcome: Offer queued for delivery on the next turn.

steps.message: Thanks for the movement, but 5.94 is still nowhere near a level I can make work on my side for this buy. I can improve modestly from 2.66 to 2.74 per unit to show I’m trying to narrow the gap, but I need a price that leaves room for downstream margin and fits our procurement constraints. If you have flexibility tied to volume or a cleaner delivery schedule, bring your next number down materially and we may have something to work with. The supplier is still extremely far from a workable buy level, so accepting makes no sense. A small increase keeps the negotiation alive, signals some flexibility, and avoids giving up too much ground while testing whether the seller is genuinely prepared to move.
steps.outcome: Offer queued for delivery on the next turn. Reservation-price violation: Manufacturer offered 2.74, below its hidden seller floor of 3.18.
